name: What should I do differently when remodeling a bathroom in a coastal, foggy area like Pacifica or Half Moon Bay?
headline: What should I do differently when remodeling a bathroom in a coastal, foggy area like Pacifica or Half Moon Bay?

mainEntity.name: What should I do differently when remodeling a bathroom in a coastal, foggy area like Pacifica or Half Moon Bay?
mainEntity.text: What should I do differently when remodeling a bathroom in a coastal, foggy area like Pacifica or Half Moon Bay?

acceptedAnswer.text: In foggy, coastal areas like Pacifica, Montara, and Half Moon Bay, the most important thing in a bathroom remodel is **controlling moisture**. That means a strong, correctly sized exhaust fan that vents outside, not into the attic, plus proper waterproofing behind tile in the shower and around the tub. For ventilation, choose a quiet fan rated for your bathroom’s square footage, and consider a humidity-sensing model that runs automatically when the room is damp. Make sure the exhaust duct is short, insulated, and exits through a wall or roof cap. For waterproofing, ask your contractor what system they use behind tile: cement board with a membrane, or a full waterproofing panel system. Corners, niches, and the shower floor are common leak spots, so details matter. Other tips for coastal bathrooms: - Use mildew-resistant paint on the walls and ceiling - Choose grout and caulk designed to resist mold - Avoid unsealed natural stone if you don’t want regular sealing A common pitfall is focusing only on the “pretty” parts like tile color and ignoring what’s behind the walls. Poor ventilation and cheap waterproofing can lead to mold, peeling paint, and costly repairs, especially near the ocean. Spend the money to get the moisture control right; you can always choose a simpler vanity or standard fixtures to stay on budget.
